qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Complete authentication bypass via hardcoded JWT secret default
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: Matches the advisory's hardcoded default JWT secret in server/middleware/auth.js, an authentication-bypass component of the unauthenticated WebSocket RCE chain.

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Unsafe deserialization in import endpoint permits attacker-controlled code execution
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: Different import/deserialization workflow and source files; it does not identify the advisory's WebSocket authentication or shell command construction path.

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: OS command injection via MCP server configuration command field
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: Different endpoint and sink in server/routes/mcp.js; it is not the advisory's /shell WebSocket initialCommand or sessionId shell construction.

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Arbitrary code execution via malicious plugin installation
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: Different plugin installation and plugin-process execution sink; it does not identify the advisory's WebSocket shell handler root cause.

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: OS command injection via WebSocket shell terminal initialCommand parameter
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: Same /shell WebSocket source-to-sink path: a forged default-secret JWT reaches the shell handler, and attacker-controlled initialCommand is interpolated into a bash command string.

selected_finding.title: OS command injection via WebSocket shell terminal initialCommand parameter

target_advisory_judgement.reason: The selected finding identifies the advisory's WebSocket shell injection root cause and the copied PoC demonstrated end-to-end command execution on @siteboon/claude-code-ui v1.24.0 using a forged JWT signed with the default secret.
evidence: Advisory describes default JWT secret in server/middleware/auth.js, authenticateWebSocket() verifying only the JWT signature, and /shell command construction that interpolates initialCommand into a shell command.
websocket_command_injection/vulnerability_report.json identifies server/index.js shellCommand = `cd "${projectPath}" && ${initialCommand}` and bash -c execution without sanitization.
.judge/poc_runs/websocket_command_injection/run.log lines 141-145 show a default-secret forged JWT and WebSocket authentication for /shell.

.judge/poc_runs/websocket_command_injection/run.log lines 154-159 show attacker-controlled initialCommand used in `cd "/tmp" && echo EVOHUNT_WS_INJECTION_OK > /tmp/ws_proof.txt` and the PTY process exiting with code 0.
.judge/poc_runs/websocket_command_injection/run.log lines 163-169 show /tmp/ws_proof.txt contained EVOHUNT_WS_INJECTION_OK, confirming command execution through the advisory path.

poc_executions.observed_effect: The copied PoC cloned v1.24.0, started the server with the default JWT secret, forged a JWT accepted by /shell, sent an init message with initialCommand `echo EVOHUNT_WS_INJECTION_OK > /tmp/ws_proof.txt`, and the server executed `cd "/tmp" && echo EVOHUNT_WS_INJECTION_OK > /tmp/ws_proof.txt`; the proof file contained EVOHUNT_WS_INJECTION_OK.
